body: SLS22 S5 delivery -- read this fully, one step is blocked and needs you. BUILD: both new modules (SEG, ERM) complete, all three compile gates (S1/S2/S3) passed clean -- 0 errors each time, only the one pre-existing baseline warning (unrelated sesMap flow-analysis false positive). Version bumped SLS21->SLS22 in header/banners/init strings; historical fix-tag comments left untouched. Files delivered: TUNGSTEN_9.43_A7.5_QUANT_SLS(22).mq4 and the compiled .ex4, both in files/from-agent. DATA PIPELINE: TUNGSTEN_events.csv seeded now with real researched USD/JPY events through Jul 10; tungsten-events-refresh skill created; TungstenEventsRefresh scheduled task registered (weekly Mondays 6am). S4 BLOCKED -- NEEDS YOU: I tried to run a fresh full calibration + ghost validation on a demo account (confirmed non-live: account 22650819 on XMGlobal-Demo, USDJPY M5) by launching the MT4 terminal headlessly with a /config StartUp directive naming the new SLS(22) build. Twice, MT4 ignored it and restored its last-saved chart state instead, which still had the OLD SLS(20) build attached -- even after trying a fresh /profile name. I have no UI-automation/screenshot tool to click through this manually, so I stopped guessing at CLI flags (each failed attempt was running real EA logic on the demo account, placing demo orders I didn't intend). Terminal is now stopped, system is clean, no live-account risk at any point -- confirmed the cached account is demo before touching anything. What I need from you: either (a) open that USDJPY M5 chart in MT4 yourself and drag the compiled TUNGSTEN_9.43_A7.5_QUANT_SLS(22).ex4 onto it (30 seconds), after which I'll pick up monitoring the calibration/ghost logs automatically and report the verdict, or (b) tell me a CLI/automation approach for this specific MT4 build (XM Global, build 1470) that actually overrides the last-saved chart's attached EA, if you know one. Either way, I did first clear the stale TW917_USDJPY_P5_PARTIAL.dat leftover state file so whichever run happens next will be a genuinely fresh calibration, not a resume. Everything else (S1, S2, S3, delivery) is done and verified; S4's calibration numbers (WR/PF/IC deltas/ghost verdict/speedup) are the only thing I can't report yet because that one step needs a human hand on the chart.
